title: Count and surface spine fail-open events — 76 silent 'mandatory predecessor artifact missing' WARNs in one batch is an epidemic without a dashboard
summary: Width-3 batch 2026-07-13: 76 occurrences of '[orchestrator] WARN spine not satisfied for next=<phase> (a mandatory predecessor's handoff artifact is missing); proceeding fail-open (would-block at enforce)'. Phases are routinely completing WITHOUT their handoff artifacts (correlates with 29 exit=85 quota bounces mid-phase — the artifact is lost when a driver bounces), and fail-open makes it invisible: no counter, no dossier field, no threshold. Before any enforce flip is thinkable, add telemetry: per-cycle count of spine fail-opens with (phase, missing artifact) pairs in the dossier; a batch-level rollup in the loop summary; WARN escalation when a single cycle exceeds a threshold (e.g. 3). This is measurement-first (token-telemetry precedent): make the epidemic visible, then decide whether the fix is artifact-retry, driver-bounce recovery, or enforce.
